commit.message:
fix: unescape collapsed code — LLM double-escaped newlines in edits

LLMs often produce \\n instead of \n in JSON edit descriptions,
causing multiline code to be collapsed to a single line with literal
\n characters. This broke ALL models in QA testing.

New _unescape_collapsed_code() uses a state machine to convert literal
\n → real newlines ONLY outside Python string literals, preserving
\n inside strings (e.g. '\n'.join()) where it belongs.

Applied in _act_edit_file() and _replan_edit() after JSON parsing.
17 new tests cover basic, mixed, triple-quoted, and realistic cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix COM reentrancy contract violations and re-enable the ExtensionPoints test (#126571)

This PR fixes the `Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints` failure by correcting
CoreCLR COM interop contract handling for paths that can legitimately
re-enter managed code during COM allocation hooks, and it includes a
small cleanup of the related COM visibility helpers. Now that the test
infrastructure change has merged, it also re-enables the
`ExtensionPoints` IMallocSpy test.

## Changes

- Keep `OleVariant::ClearLPSTRArray` and `OleVariant::ClearLPWSTRArray`
as `NOTHROW`, but mark the `CoTaskMemFree` cleanup paths with a
permanent `ThrowsViolation` for runtime reentrancy, since
`CoTaskMemFree` can invoke a managed `IMallocSpy` implementation.
- Wrap the `ComPreStubWorker` setup around the managed exception
dispatcher and unwind handler in a
`BEGIN_CONTRACT_VIOLATION(ThrowsViolation)` / `END_CONTRACT_VIOLATION`
block.
- Update the outer static contract on `ComPreStubWorker` to
`STATIC_CONTRACT_NOTHROW` so it matches the intended HRESULT-returning
behavior outside the explicit violation scope.
- Remove the unused `fForIDispatch` parameter from
`CheckParentComVisibility`.
- Delete the redundant `CheckParentComVisibilityNoThrow` helper and use
`HasInvisibleParent()` directly at the remaining visibility check.
- Re-enable `src/tests/Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints/ExtensionPoints.cs`
by removing the temporary `ActiveIssue` skip on
`Validate_Managed_IMallocSpy`.

## Why this addresses the failure

The failing test exercises COM extension points that can callback into
managed code during allocation cleanup and stub initialization. Those
paths were operating under overly strict contracts, which made valid
reentrant behavior show up as contract violations. This change narrows
the violation handling to the specific reentrant regions while keeping
the surrounding HRESULT-returning contracts intact. The
visibility-helper refactors are supporting cleanups with no intended
behavioral change, and re-enabling the test ensures the original failure
scenario is covered again now that the test can run.

---------

fix: pre-create all TLD partitions at worker startup to prevent DDL/DML lock deadlock

Running CREATE TABLE ... PARTITION OF domain during the hot-path (concurrent
with INSERT batches from another container) requires ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the
parent domain table, which queued behind ROW EXCLUSIVE locks from ongoing
INSERTs — causing a full deadlock that stalled both containers.

Fix: _prewarm_all_partitions() runs at main() startup before run_sync_cycle(),
creating any missing partitions while no concurrent ingest is active. From that
point, ensure_partition() inside the per-TLD hot-path is always a no-op
(partition already exists), so no DDL ever contends with live INSERT batches.

Introduce LedgerSeq newtype for ledger sequence numbers

Replace raw u32 with a LedgerSeq newtype wrapper across the entire workspace.
The type provides type safety at ledger sequence boundaries while maintaining
ergonomic arithmetic and comparison operations.

Key design decisions:
- Private inner field with explicit get()/new() for XDR boundary conversions
- From<u32>/Into<u32> for ergonomic conversions
- Arithmetic ops (Add/Sub/Rem with u32) to avoid .get() in common patterns
- PartialEq<u32>/PartialOrd<u32> for direct comparison
- Serde transparent, rusqlite ToSql/FromSql behind feature flag
- No Div (use .get() first), no tracing::Value (sealed trait)

113 files changed across all workspace crates.

Closes #1226

commit.message:
Fix view toggle missing from mobile overflow menu on iOS

The card/list view toggle was not rendering in the SecondaryActions
dropdown on MAUI Blazor Hybrid (iOS WebView). Root cause: inline
ternary expressions in Razor markup (@onclick and @() class/text
bindings) caused the MAUI WebView Blazor renderer to silently drop
the entire <li> element from the render tree.

Fix: extract the ternary logic into computed properties
(ViewToggleIcon, ViewToggleLabel) and a ToggleViewMode method,
keeping the markup simple enough for the WebView renderer.

Verified on iOS 26.2 simulator via DevFlow CDP inspection —
the menu now shows all items including 'List View' toggle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace combinedClickable on Surface with pointerInput(detectTapGestures)
on the outer Box container. combinedClickable uses PointerEventPass.Main
(bottom-up), so ClickableText composables inside MarkdownContent (which
also use Main pass) intercepted the gesture first and prevented the
long-press from being detected reliably on tall/rich assistant messages.

detectTapGestures calls awaitFirstDown(requireUnconsumed = false), so
the outer Box receives the down event regardless of child consumption.
Long-press is detected via a delay timeout — independent of whether
inner ClickableText nodes later consume pointer events.

User messages (plain Text, no ClickableText) were unaffected; this fix
makes assistant message long-press parity consistent across all content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- pointerInput now only applied to assistant messages (user messages keep

- onPress handler emits PressInteraction to interactionSource so ripple

- GPR occupancy sweet spot: 16-32 GPR per kernel
- KCACHE/LDS crash confirmed as ALU-IS-LDS bug (already fixed)

Fix: align watchdog worker panels and stop false non-interactive tool skips

Root causes:
- Worker panel table width was unstable due to emoji width in fixed columns, causing right-border drift.
- Tool execution was skipped whenever stdout wasn't a TTY, incorrectly blocking watchdog/runtime execution.
- Spinner thread logs were tagged as W0 because thread-local worker context was not propagated.

Changes:
- core/ui.py:
  * Worker table now uses ASCII-only tool labels in fixed-width column (stable panel borders).
  * Keeps tactical symbols/progress while preventing Unicode-width misalignment.
- recon/engines.py:
  * Removed hard skip based on isatty/TERM.
  * Skip now only in explicit test/disabled contexts:
    - HUNT3R_DISABLE_TOOL_EXECUTION=1
    - PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST set
  * Applies to run_cmd() and run_nuclei().
- core/scanner.py:
  * _run_with_progress() now captures current worker_id and sets thread-local context
    inside spinner thread, so runtime logs are attributed to the correct worker.

Result:
- Panels stay aligned.
- Watchdog can execute tools in service/non-interactive sessions instead of ENGINE_SKIP loops.
- Activity log shows per-worker progress lines with correct worker IDs.

commit.message:
fix: graceful fallback when GitHub rejects inline review comments (#50)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 because the CLI generated inline comments
with line numbers not in the PR diff. The entire review was lost.

## Fix

When `post_review()` gets a 422 and has inline comments, it retries
without them — appending the comment text to the review body instead.
The review still gets posted, feedback isn't lost.

## Context

This is a natural consequence of the token refactor (#40) — the CLI
writes review JSON and the orchestrator posts it. The CLI doesn't know
which lines are in the diff, so it may guess wrong. This fallback
ensures the review always gets posted.

* refactor: replace pycares private API with public aiodns.DNSResolver warmup

Use aiodns.DNSResolver(loop=loop) instead of pycares._shutdown_manager.start()
to trigger Thread-1 initialisation before verify_cleanup's baseline snapshot.

- coordinator.py: use getattr fallback for async_create_background_task so the
  integration works on older HA cores that only have async_create_task
- test_webhook.py: call await resp.release() after every HTTP response to avoid
  resource warnings and aiohttp cleanup errors

  with accurate description of the aiodns.DNSResolver() warmup approach

* fix: replace await resp.release() with await resp.read() in webhook tests

ClientResponse.release() is not a coroutine in aiohttp 3.x; use resp.read()
which is always a proper coroutine and correctly consumes the response body.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PGEviUQynUPGCdWnJ9MDKT

* fix: use wait_background_tasks=True in auto-clear tests to avoid race condition

Background tasks created via async_create_background_task are intentionally
not awaited by hass.async_block_till_done(). The two auto-clear tests were
relying on a single async_block_till_done() call to complete the auto-clear
task, which was racey. Fix: add wait_background_tasks=True so HA drains the
background task queue, followed by a second async_block_till_done() to flush
any HA state-write callbacks the coordinator triggers.

Also update TESTING.md to document the correct two-call pattern for auto-clear
integration tests.

commit.message:
fix: register SIGTERM handler before initial sync cycle

Move signal handler registration to the top of main() so that
SIGTERM sent by Docker during the initial startup sync (which can
run for hours across 300+ TLDs) is correctly intercepted.

Previously the handler was only registered after the blocking
scheduler was created, meaning containers receiving SIGTERM during
the startup cycle would ignore it and keep running until SIGKILL
(30-minute grace period). This caused 3 simultaneous worker
containers during rolling deploys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
